mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in the federal funds rate increases the risk-free rate and equity discount rates, reducing present values and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations during periods of rising FEDFUNDS over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, rising joblessness signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate collapsing demand and supply-side spillovers that jointly amplify downside risk to corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observed months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase the federal funds rate, raising real discount rates and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During monetary tightening cycles, the correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and 60-day S&P 500 returns is non-negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in industrial production signal immediate factory output contraction and inventory liquidation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in nonfarm employment signal weakening labor income and consumption demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in PAYEMS are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal improving construction activity and forward-looking household formation, lifting cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST above 1000 is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates erodes real earnings growth and raises recession probability, jointly driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months where CPIAUCSL exceeds its 12-month trailing average and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.
